# solution.py
"""TeamCollaborationManager -- complete stdlib-only implementation."""

import csv
import dataclasses
import datetime
import enum
import io
import json
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enums
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TaskStatus(enum.Enum):
    """Status values for a task lifecycle."""

    NOT_STARTED = "NOT_STARTED"
    IN_PROGRESS = "IN_PROGRESS"
    COMPLETED = "COMPLETED"


class UserRole(enum.Enum):
    """Role values for a registered user."""

    MEMBER = "MEMBER"
    ADMIN = "ADMIN"


class ReportFormat(enum.Enum):
    """Supported export formats for reports."""

    CSV = "CSV"
    PDF = "PDF"


class ReportType(enum.Enum):
    """Type identifiers used in report dicts."""

    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "TEAM_PERFORMANCE"
    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "PROJECT_PROGRESS"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exceptions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base exception for all team-collaboration errors."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a requested entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when the caller is not permitted to perform the action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a unique constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Dataclasses
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attachment associated with a Message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime.datetime


@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered user in the system."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime.datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A project with a defined date range and owner."""

    id: int
    name: str
    start_date: datetime.datetime
    end_date: datetime.datetime
    owner_id: int
    description: str = ""


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work within a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A message posted to a project or task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    edited_at: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating submitted by one user for another."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime.datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_UNSET = object()


def _clone_attachment(att):
    """Return a shallow copy of an Attachment (all fields are immutable)."""
    return dataclasses.replace(att)


def _clone_msg(msg):
    """Return a copy of a Message with a fresh attachments list."""
    new_atts = [_clone_attachment(a) for a in msg.attachments]
    return dataclasses.replace(msg, attachments=new_atts)


def _clone(obj):
    """Return a copy of a flat dataclass or a Message."""
    if isinstance(obj, Message):
        return _clone_msg(obj)
    return dataclasses.replace(obj)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal sub-stores (each exposes _lock pointing at the shared RLock)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class _UserStore:
    """Internal store for User entities."""

    def __init__(self, lock):
        """Initialise with the shared RLock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._users = {}
        self._by_username = {}
        self._by_email = {}


class _ProjectStore:
    """Internal store for Project entities."""

    def __init__(self, lock):
        """Initialise with the shared RLock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._projects = {}
        self._by_owner = {}


class _TaskStore:
    """Internal store for Task entities."""

    def __init__(self, lock):
        """Initialise with the shared RLock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._tasks = {}
        self._by_project = {}
        self._by_assignee = {}


class _MessageStore:
    """Internal store for Message entities."""

    def __init__(self, lock):
        """Initialise with the shared RLock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._messages = {}
        self._by_project = {}
        self._by_task = {}


class _PerformanceStore:
    """Internal store for Rating entities."""

    def __init__(self, lock):
        """Initialise with the shared RLock."""
        self._lock = lock
        self._ratings = {}
        self._by_ratee = {}


class _ReportStore:
    """Placeholder store for the report service (no persistent state)."""

    def __init__(self, lock):
        """Initialise with the shared RLock."""
        self._lock = lock


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Central manager for users, projects, tasks, messages, performance, and reports."""

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        """Initialise the manager with an optional injectable clock callable."""
        if now is None:
            now = datetime.datetime.now
        self._now = now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        self.users = _UserStore(self._lock)
        self.projects = _ProjectStore(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _TaskStore(self._lock)
        self.messages = _MessageStore(self._lock)
        self.performance = _PerformanceStore(self._lock)
        self.reports = _ReportStore(self._lock)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Internal helpers (caller must hold self._lock)
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _next_id(self):
        """Return the next globally unique monotonic id."""
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # User management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        """Register a new user and return the User. Raises on validation or duplicates."""
        with self._lock:
            username = username.strip()
            if not username:
                raise ValidationError("Username cannot be empty.")
            if not email or "@" not in email:
                raise ValidationError("Email must contain '@'.")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("Role must be a UserRole instance.")
            u_lower = username.lower()
            e_lower = email.lower()
            if u_lower in self.users._by_username:
                raise DuplicateError("Username already taken.")
            if e_lower in self.users._by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("Email already registered.")
            uid = self._next_id()
            user = User(id=uid, username=username, email=email, role=role,
                        created_at=self._now())
            self.users._users[uid] = user
            self.users._by_username[u_lower] = uid
            self.users._by_email[e_lower] = uid
            return _clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        """Return a copy of the User with the given id, or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._lock:
            u = self.users._users.get(user_id)
            if u is None:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            return _clone(u)

    def list_users(self):
        """Return all users sorted by id."""
        with self._lock:
            return [_clone(u) for u in sorted(self.users._users.values(),
                                               key=lambda x: x.id)]

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=_UNSET, email=_UNSET, role=_UNSET):
        """Update user fields; omitted args are unchanged. Returns updated User."""
        with self._lock:
            u = self.users._users.get(user_id)
            if u is None:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            new_username = u.username if username is _UNSET else username
            new_email = u.email if email is _UNSET else email
            new_role = u.role if role is _UNSET else role
            if new_username is not None:
                new_username = new_username.strip()
                if not new_username:
                    raise ValidationError("Username cannot be empty.")
            if new_email is not None and "@" not in new_email:
                raise ValidationError("Email must contain '@'.")
            u_lower = new_username.lower()
            e_lower = new_email.lower()
            existing_u = self.users._by_username.get(u_lower)
            if existing_u is not None and existing_u != user_id:
                raise DuplicateError("Username already taken.")
            existing_e = self.users._by_email.get(e_lower)
            if existing_e is not None and existing_e != user_id:
                raise DuplicateError("Email already registered.")
            old_u_lower = u.username.lower()
            old_e_lower = u.email.lower()
            if old_u_lower != u_lower:
                del self.users._by_username[old_u_lower]
                self.users._by_username[u_lower] = user_id
            if old_e_lower != e_lower:
                del self.users._by_email[old_e_lower]
                self.users._by_email[e_lower] = user_id
            updated = dataclasses.replace(u, username=new_username,
                                          email=new_email, role=new_role)
            self.users._users[user_id] = updated
            return _clone(updated)

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        """Delete a user. Raises ConflictError if they own a project or have open tasks."""
        with self._lock:
            u = self.users._users.get(user_id)
            if u is None:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            if self.projects._by_owner.get(user_id):
                raise ConflictError("User owns one or more projects.")
            task_ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, [])
            for tid in task_ids:
                t = self.tasks._tasks.get(tid)
                if t is not None and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("User has non-completed assigned tasks.")
            del self.users._by_username[u.username.lower()]
            del self.users._by_email[u.email.lower()]
            del self.users._users[user_id]
            if user_id in self.tasks._by_assignee:
                del self.tasks._by_assignee[user_id]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Project management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""):
        """Create and return a new Project."""
        with self._lock:
            if owner_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Owner {0} not found.".format(owner_id))
            if not name or not name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Project name cannot be empty.")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must be >= start_date.")
            pid = self._next_id()
            proj = Project(id=pid, name=name, start_date=start_date,
                           end_date=end_date, owner_id=owner_id,
                           description=description)
            self.projects._projects[pid] = proj
            self.projects._by_owner.setdefault(owner_id, set()).add(pid)
            return _clone(proj)

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        """Return a copy of the Project, or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._lock:
            p = self.projects._projects.get(project_id)
            if p is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            return _clone(p)

    def list_projects(self):
        """Return all projects sorted by id."""
        with self._lock:
            return [_clone(p) for p in sorted(self.projects._projects.values(),
                                               key=lambda x: x.id)]

    def update_project(self, project_id, name=_UNSET, start_date=_UNSET,
                       end_date=_UNSET, description=_UNSET):
        """Update project fields; omitted args are unchanged. Returns updated Project."""
        with self._lock:
            p = self.projects._projects.get(project_id)
            if p is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            new_name = p.name if name is _UNSET else name
            new_start = p.start_date if start_date is _UNSET else start_date
            new_end = p.end_date if end_date is _UNSET else end_date
            new_desc = p.description if description is _UNSET else description
            if new_name is not None and (not new_name or not new_name.strip()):
                raise ValidationError("Project name cannot be empty.")
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must be >= start_date.")
            updated = dataclasses.replace(p, name=new_name, start_date=new_start,
                                          end_date=new_end, description=new_desc)
            self.projects._projects[project_id] = updated
            return _clone(updated)

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        """Delete a project and cascade-delete its tasks and messages."""
        with self._lock:
            p = self.projects._projects.get(project_id)
            if p is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            # Cascade: remove tasks (and their messages, assignee index entries)
            task_ids = list(self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, []))
            for tid in task_ids:
                t = self.tasks._tasks.get(tid)
                if t is not None:
                    if t.assignee_id is not None and t.assignee_id in self.tasks._by_assignee:
                        self.tasks._by_assignee[t.assignee_id].discard(tid)
                    # Remove task-scoped messages
                    m_ids = list(self.messages._by_task.get(tid, []))
                    for mid in m_ids:
                        self.messages._messages.pop(mid, None)
                    self.messages._by_task.pop(tid, None)
                    del self.tasks._tasks[tid]
            self.tasks._by_project.pop(project_id, None)
            # Cascade: remove project-scoped messages
            m_ids = list(self.messages._by_project.get(project_id, []))
            for mid in m_ids:
                self.messages._messages.pop(mid, None)
            self.messages._by_project.pop(project_id, None)
            # Remove from owner index
            owner_set = self.projects._by_owner.get(p.owner_id, set())
            owner_set.discard(project_id)
            del self.projects._projects[project_id]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Task management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None):
        """Create and return a new Task within the given project."""
        with self._lock:
            p = self.projects._projects.get(project_id)
            if p is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            if assignee_id is not None and assignee_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Assignee {0} not found.".format(assignee_id))
            if not title or not title.strip():
                raise ValidationError("Task title cannot be empty.")
            if deadline is not None and deadline < p.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("Deadline must be >= project start_date.")
            tid = self._next_id()
            task = Task(id=tid, project_id=project_id, title=title,
                        status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED, assignee_id=assignee_id,
                        deadline=deadline)
            self.tasks._tasks[tid] = task
            self.tasks._by_project.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(tid)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self.tasks._by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(tid)
            return _clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        """Return a copy of the Task, or raise NotFoundError."""
        with self._lock:
            t = self.tasks._tasks.get(task_id)
            if t is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            return _clone(t)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        """Return tasks for the project sorted by id. Raises NotFoundError if missing."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id not in self.projects._projects:
                raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            ids = self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, set())
            valid_ids = sorted(i for i in ids if i in self.tasks._tasks)
            return [_clone(self.tasks._tasks[i]) for i in valid_ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id):
        """Return tasks assigned to the user sorted by id. Raises NotFoundError if missing."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
            valid_ids = sorted(i for i in ids if i in self.tasks._tasks)
            return [_clone(self.tasks._tasks[i]) for i in valid_ids]

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        """Assign a task to a user, updating the assignee index."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self.tasks._tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            if assignee_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Assignee {0} not found.".format(assignee_id))
            old_asgn = task.assignee_id
            if old_asgn is not None and old_asgn != assignee_id:
                if old_asgn in self.tasks._by_assignee:
                    self.tasks._by_assignee[old_asgn].discard(task_id)
            updated = dataclasses.replace(task, assignee_id=assignee_id)
            self.tasks._tasks[task_id] = updated
            self.tasks._by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task_id)
            return _clone(updated)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id):
        """Reassign a task to a different user."""
        with self._lock:
            if task_id not in self.tasks._tasks:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            if new_assignee_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Assignee {0} not found.".format(new_assignee_id))
            return self.assign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        """Remove the assignee from a task."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self.tasks._tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            old_asgn = task.assignee_id
            if old_asgn is not None and old_asgn in self.tasks._by_assignee:
                self.tasks._by_assignee[old_asgn].discard(task_id)
            updated = dataclasses.replace(task, assignee_id=None)
            self.tasks._tasks[task_id] = updated
            return _clone(updated)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        """Set or clear the deadline for a task."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self.tasks._tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            if deadline is not None:
                p = self.projects._projects.get(task.project_id)
                if p is not None and deadline < p.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("Deadline must be >= project start_date.")
            updated = dataclasses.replace(task, deadline=deadline)
            self.tasks._tasks[task_id] = updated
            return _clone(updated)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        """Update task status with appropriate timestamp transitions."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self.tasks._tasks.get(task_id)
            if task is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("Status must be a TaskStatus instance.")
            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                updated = dataclasses.replace(task, status=status,
                                              started_at=None, completed_at=None)
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                started_at = task.started_at if task.started_at is not None else now
                updated = dataclasses.replace(task, status=status,
                                              started_at=started_at,
                                              completed_at=None)
            else:  # COMPLETED
                started_at = task.started_at if task.started_at is not None else now
                updated = dataclasses.replace(task, status=status,
                                              started_at=started_at,
                                              completed_at=now)
            self.tasks._tasks[task_id] = updated
            return _clone(updated)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Messaging
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def post_message(self, author_id, body, project_id=None, task_id=None,
                     parent_id=None, attachments=None):
        """Post a message to a project or task. Exactly one target required."""
        with self._lock:
            if author_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Author {0} not found.".format(author_id))
            if project_id is None and task_id is None:
                raise ValidationError("Specify exactly one of project_id or task_id.")
            if project_id is not None and task_id is not None:
                raise ValidationError("Specify only one of project_id or task_id.")
            if project_id is not None:
                if project_id not in self.projects._projects:
                    raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            else:
                if task_id not in self.tasks._tasks:
                    raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            atts_list = attachments or []
            if not body and not atts_list:
                raise ValidationError("Body cannot be empty without attachments.")
            if parent_id is not None:
                if parent_id not in self.messages._messages:
                    raise NotFoundError("Parent message {0} not found.".format(parent_id))
            now = self._now()
            built_atts = [Attachment(filename=fname, size=len(data),
                                     uploaded_by=author_id, uploaded_at=now)
                          for fname, data in atts_list]
            mid = self._next_id()
            msg = Message(id=mid, author_id=author_id, body=body,
                          project_id=project_id, task_id=task_id,
                          parent_id=parent_id, attachments=built_atts)
            self.messages._messages[mid] = msg
            if project_id is not None:
                self.messages._by_project.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(mid)
            else:
                self.messages._by_task.setdefault(task_id, set()).add(mid)
            return _clone_msg(msg)

    def edit_message(self, msg_id, editor_id, new_body):
        """Edit the body of a message. Only the original author may edit."""
        with self._lock:
            m = self.messages._messages.get(msg_id)
            if m is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found.".format(msg_id))
            if m.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author can edit this message.")
            if not new_body:
                raise ValidationError("New body cannot be empty.")
            updated = dataclasses.replace(m, body=new_body, edited_at=self._now())
            self.messages._messages[msg_id] = updated
            return _clone_msg(updated)

    def add_attachment(self, msg_id, user_id, filename, data):
        """Add an attachment to a message. Only the author may add attachments."""
        with self._lock:
            m = self.messages._messages.get(msg_id)
            if m is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found.".format(msg_id))
            if m.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author can add attachments.")
            if not filename:
                raise ValidationError("Filename cannot be empty.")
            att = Attachment(filename=filename, size=len(data),
                             uploaded_by=user_id, uploaded_at=self._now())
            new_atts = list(m.attachments) + [att]
            updated = dataclasses.replace(m, attachments=new_atts)
            self.messages._messages[msg_id] = updated
            return _clone_msg(updated)

    def delete_message(self, msg_id, user_id):
        """Delete a message. Only the original author may delete."""
        with self._lock:
            m = self.messages._messages.get(msg_id)
            if m is None:
                raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found.".format(msg_id))
            if m.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author can delete this message.")
            del self.messages._messages[msg_id]
            if m.project_id is not None and m.project_id in self.messages._by_project:
                self.messages._by_project[m.project_id].discard(msg_id)
            if m.task_id is not None and m.task_id in self.messages._by_task:
                self.messages._by_task[m.task_id].discard(msg_id)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        """Return messages for a project sorted by id. Raises NotFoundError if missing."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id not in self.projects._projects:
                raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            ids = self.messages._by_project.get(project_id, set())
            valid_ids = sorted(i for i in ids if i in self.messages._messages)
            return [_clone_msg(self.messages._messages[i]) for i in valid_ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        """Return messages for a task sorted by id. Raises NotFoundError if missing."""
        with self._lock:
            if task_id not in self.tasks._tasks:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            ids = self.messages._by_task.get(task_id, set())
            valid_ids = sorted(i for i in ids if i in self.messages._messages)
            return [_clone_msg(self.messages._messages[i]) for i in valid_ids]

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Performance
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        """Return the fraction of assigned tasks that are COMPLETED (0.0 if none)."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, [])
            tasks = [self.tasks._tasks[i] for i in ids if i in self.tasks._tasks]
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        """Return average seconds to complete assigned tasks, or None if none completed."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, [])
            tasks = [self.tasks._tasks[i] for i in ids if i in self.tasks._tasks]
            done = [t for t in tasks
                    if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and t.started_at is not None
                    and t.completed_at is not None]
            if not done:
                return None
            total_secs = sum((t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds() for t in done)
            return total_secs / len(done)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="", task_id=None):
        """Submit a rating from rater to ratee. Score must be 1-5."""
        with self._lock:
            # Score validation BEFORE existence lookups (spec-pinned order)
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int) or not (1 <= score <= 5):
                raise ValidationError("Score must be an integer between 1 and 5.")
            if rater_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Rater {0} not found.".format(rater_id))
            if ratee_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("Ratee {0} not found.".format(ratee_id))
            if task_id is not None and task_id not in self.tasks._tasks:
                raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found.".format(task_id))
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("Cannot submit a rating for yourself.")
            rid = self._next_id()
            rating = Rating(id=rid, rater_id=rater_id, ratee_id=ratee_id,
                            score=score, feedback=feedback, task_id=task_id,
                            created_at=self._now())
            self.performance._ratings[rid] = rating
            self.performance._by_ratee.setdefault(ratee_id, []).append(rid)
            return _clone(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        """Return a dict with count, average, and list of Rating objects for a user."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            rids = self.performance._by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            ratings = [self.performance._ratings[r] for r in rids
                       if r in self.performance._ratings]
            count = len(ratings)
            average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count > 0 else 0.0
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [_clone(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        """Return an aggregated performance dashboard dict for a user."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, [])
            tasks = [self.tasks._tasks[i] for i in ids if i in self.tasks._tasks]
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = 0
            in_progress = 0
            not_started = 0
            done_tasks = []
            for t in tasks:
                if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                    if t.started_at is not None and t.completed_at is not None:
                        done_tasks.append(t)
                elif t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                    in_progress += 1
                else:
                    not_started += 1
            rate = completed / total if total > 0 else 0.0
            avg_time = None
            if done_tasks:
                avg_time = (sum((t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
                                for t in done_tasks) / len(done_tasks))
            rids = self.performance._by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            ratings = [self.performance._ratings[r] for r in rids
                       if r in self.performance._ratings]
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                              if rating_count > 0 else 0.0)
            return {
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": rate,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Reports
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        """Return a progress report dict for a project."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id not in self.projects._projects:
                raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            ids = self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, [])
            tasks = [self.tasks._tasks[i] for i in ids if i in self.tasks._tasks]
            total = len(tasks)
            status_counts = {
                TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED.value: 0,
                TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value: 0,
                TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value: 0,
            }
            for t in tasks:
                status_counts[t.status.value] += 1
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            rate = completed / total if total > 0 else 0.0
            now = self._now()
            overdue = sum(1 for t in tasks
                          if t.deadline is not None
                          and t.deadline < now
                          and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            per_assignee = {}
            for t in tasks:
                if t.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(t.assignee_id,
                                                     {"total": 0, "completed": 0})
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1
            return {
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        """Return a team performance report dict, optionally scoped to a project."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                if project_id not in self.projects._projects:
                    raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found.".format(project_id))
            rows = []
            for uid, user in self.users._users.items():
                ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(uid, [])
                if project_id is not None:
                    tasks = [self.tasks._tasks[i] for i in ids
                             if i in self.tasks._tasks
                             and self.tasks._tasks[i].project_id == project_id]
                    if not tasks:
                        continue
                else:
                    tasks = [self.tasks._tasks[i] for i in ids
                             if i in self.tasks._tasks]
                total = len(tasks)
                completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
                rate = completed / total if total > 0 else 0.0
                rids = self.performance._by_ratee.get(uid, [])
                ratings = [self.performance._ratings[r] for r in rids
                           if r in self.performance._ratings]
                avg_rating = (sum(r.score for r in ratings) / len(ratings)
                              if ratings else 0.0)
                rows.append({
                    "user_id": uid,
                    "username": user.username,
                    "total_tasks": total,
                    "completed_tasks": completed,
                    "completion_rate": rate,
                    "average_rating": avg_rating,
                })
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        """Return a performance report dict for a single user."""
        with self._lock:
            if user_id not in self.users._users:
                raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found.".format(user_id))
            ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, [])
            tasks = [self.tasks._tasks[i] for i in ids if i in self.tasks._tasks]
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "tasks": [_clone(t) for t in tasks],
            }

    def export_report(self, report, fmt):
        """Export a report dict as CSV (str) or PDF (bytes). Raises ValidationError on bad args."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
                raise ValidationError("Format must be a ReportFormat instance.")
            if not isinstance(report, dict):
                raise ValidationError("Report must be a dict.")
            if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
                return self._to_csv(report)
            return self._to_pdf(report)

    def _to_csv(self, report):
        """Render a report dict as a CSV string."""
        buf = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buf)
        rows = report.get("rows")
        if rows is not None:
            if rows:
                keys = list(rows[0].keys())
                writer.writerow(keys)
                for row in rows:
                    writer.writerow([row.get(k, "") for k in keys])
            else:
                # No rows: write scalar keys from the report as header
                writer.writerow(list(report.keys()))
        else:
            writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
            for k, v in report.items():
                cell = json.dumps(v) if isinstance(v, (dict, list)) else str(v)
                writer.writerow([k, cell])
        return buf.getvalue()

    def _to_pdf(self, report):
        """Render a report dict as minimal valid PDF bytes starting with b'%PDF-'."""
        lines = []
        for k, v in report.items():
            raw = json.dumps(v) if isinstance(v, (dict, list)) else str(v)
            raw = raw.replace("\\", "\\\\").replace("(", "\\(").replace(")", "\\)")
            lines.append("({0}: {1}) Tj".format(k, raw))
            lines.append("0 -20 Td")

        content_ops = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 750 Td"] + lines + ["ET"]
        content = "\n".join(content_ops)
        content_bytes = content.encode("ascii")
        c_len = len(content_bytes)

        obj1 = b"1 0 obj\n<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>\nendobj\n"
        obj2 = b"2 0 obj\n<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>\nendobj\n"
        obj3 = (b"3 0 obj\n<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R "
                b"/MediaBox [0 0 612 792] /Contents 4 0 R "
                b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> >>\nendobj\n")
        obj4 = (b"4 0 obj\n<< /Length " + str(c_len).encode() +
                b" >>\nstream\n" + content_bytes + b"\nendstream\nendobj\n")
        obj5 = (b"5 0 obj\n<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 "
                b"/BaseFont /Helvetica >>\nendobj\n")

        header = b"%PDF-1.4\n"
        objects = [obj1, obj2, obj3, obj4, obj5]
        body = b""
        offsets = []
        pos = len(header)
        for obj in objects:
            offsets.append(pos)
            body += obj
            pos += len(obj)

        xref_pos = len(header) + len(body)
        xref = b"xref\n0 6\n"
        xref += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for off in offsets:
            xref += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(off).encode()
        trailer = (b"trailer\n<< /Size 6 /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
                   b"startxref\n" + str(xref_pos).encode() + b"\n%%EOF\n")

        return header + body + xref + trailer
